2606.07410.title: A Comprehensive Anatomy of Human and DeepSeek-R1 LLM Mathematical Reasoning
2606.07410.paper_summary: The emergence of "Aha moments" in large language models, particularly DeepSeek-R1-0120, has raised the question of whether these systems genuinely reason or merely imitate the appearance of reasoning. We conduct a comprehensive empirical comparison between model and human reasoning across all 30 problems from AIME 2025, exhaustively annotating 10,247 reasoning steps into five functional categories: Analysis, Inference, Branch, Backtrace, and Reflection. We find a clear structural difference. Human solutions maintain a compact alternation between analysis and deduction, whereas DeepSeek-R1 frequently revisits intermediate results, performs shallow and often unnecessary verification, and loops through local checks without meaningful logical progress. We describe this as topological mimicry: reproducing the surface form of reasoning without its functional role. Despite this, we identify two signals of genuine reasoning. First, successful traces exhibit stable use of branching and backtracking, while failed traces either underuse or overuse exploratory actions. Second, reflection is only effective when placed within deductive inference; reflections trapped in analysis loops focus on local numerical details while missing global logical errors. These findings suggest that current long-CoT models may be rewarded more for the appearance of reasoning than for genuine deductive progress. We discuss directions for improving evaluation and training, including measuring cross-trace stability, penalising "spinning-wheel" traces, encouraging deeper logical correction, and reallocating inference-time compute toward deduction and backtracking. Overall, reasoning quality depends not simply on how much reflection occurs, but on whether reflection appears consistently and at the appropriate logical scale.
2606.07410.paper_summary_zh: "Aha時刻"在大型語言模型中的出現，特別是DeepSeek-R1-0120，引發了這些系統是否真正進行推理或僅僅模仿推理外觀的問題。<lb/>我們對AIME 2025中的所有30個問題進行了全面的實證比較，將10,247個推理步驟詳細註釋為五個功能類別：分析、推斷、分支、回溯和反思。<lb/>我們發現了明顯的結構差異。<lb/>人類的解決方案在分析和推理之間保持緊湊的交替，而DeepSeek-R1則經常重訪中間結果，進行淺層且常常不必要的驗證，並在當地檢查中循環而沒有實質性的邏輯進展。<lb/>我們將這描述為拓撲模仿：再現推理的表面形式而不具備其功能角色。<lb/>儘管如此，我們確定了兩個真正推理的信號。<lb/>首先，成功的追蹤顯示出穩定使用分支和回溯，而失敗的追蹤則是低使用或過度使用探索性行動。<lb/>其次，反思只有在置於演繹推理中時才有效；被困在分析循環中的反思專注於局部數字細節，而忽略了全局邏輯錯誤。<lb/>這些發現表明，當前的長期CoT模型可能更因推理的外觀而獲得獎勵，而非真正的演繹進展。<lb/>我們討論了改進評估和訓練的方向，包括測量跨追蹤穩定性、懲罰“旋轉輪”追蹤、鼓勵更深入的邏輯修正，以及重新分配推理時間的計算資源以支持演繹和回溯。<lb/>總的來說，推理質量不僅取決於反思的頻率，還取決於反思是否持續出現並在適當的邏輯範疇內。
2606.07410.author: Yuxiang Chen et.al.
